title: gnaphalium johnstonii
intro: Gnaphalium johnstonii, also known as the Johnston's cudweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the high-altitude regions of New Zealand. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of essential oils and terpenes. The health benefits of Johnston's cudweed include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, while also promoting wound healing and soothing skin irritations. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties and support overall well-being.
